title: Permanent Residence (5 Years)
description: Who qualifies, what documents you need, and what to do after approval

exercises.title: Key Terms
exercises.subtitle: These are the terms you will see on forms and hear at the Foreign Police. Learn them.

scenarios.situation: You are a non-EU citizen married to a Slovak citizen who has permanent residence in Slovakia. Can you apply for 5-year permanent residence?
choices: No — you must first have temporary residence for 5 years
Yes — being married to a Slovak citizen with permanent residence is one of the qualifying conditions
Yes — but only after 2 years of marriage
No — only EU citizens can apply for permanent residence
scenarios.answer: Yes — being married to a Slovak citizen with permanent residence is one of the qualifying conditions
scenarios.answerMeaning: Marriage to a Slovak citizen who holds permanent residence in Slovakia is a direct qualifying condition for 5-year permanent residence. You do not need to wait 5 years — this is a separate faster route.

scenarios.situation: You qualify for 5-year permanent residence. You are currently in Slovakia on a valid temporary residence permit. Where do you submit your application?
choices: At the Slovak embassy in your home country only
At any police station in Slovakia
At the Foreign Police in Slovakia — because you already hold a valid residence permit
By post to the Ministry of Interior
scenarios.answer: At the Foreign Police in Slovakia — because you already hold a valid residence permit
scenarios.answerMeaning: The default is to apply at the Slovak embassy abroad. However, if you are already legally present in Slovakia on a valid permit, tolerated stay, or valid visa, you can apply directly at the Foreign Police in Slovakia.

scenarios.situation: You submit your application but one document is missing. What happens?
choices: The officer accepts it and you bring the missing document later
The application is not accepted — you receive a written list of missing documents
Your application is automatically rejected
You have 7 days to bring the missing document
scenarios.answer: The application is not accepted — you receive a written list of missing documents
scenarios.answerMeaning: An incomplete application is not accepted at all. The Foreign Police or embassy will give you a written list of exactly which documents are missing so you can prepare a complete application.

scenarios.situation: Your criminal record was issued 95 days ago. Can you use it for your application?
choices: Yes — 95 days is close enough
Yes — criminal records have no expiry for residence applications
No — documents cannot be older than 90 days
Only if it has an apostille
scenarios.answer: No — documents cannot be older than 90 days
scenarios.answerMeaning: Documents attached to your application cannot be older than 90 days — the only exception is civil registry documents such as a marriage certificate. A criminal record issued 95 days ago will be rejected.

scenarios.situation: You lived in Germany for 8 months two years ago. Which criminal record(s) do you need for your Slovak application?
choices: Only from your home country
Only from Slovakia
From your home country AND from Germany
From your home country, Germany, and Slovakia
scenarios.answer: From your home country AND from Germany
scenarios.answerMeaning: You need a criminal record from your home country AND from any country where you spent more than 90 days during 6 consecutive months in the last 3 years. 8 months in Germany qualifies — you need both.

exercises.title: At the Foreign Police — Key Phrases
exercises.subtitle: What you will say and hear at your permanent residence appointment.

rows.label: Opening
rows.slovak: Chcel/a by som požiadať o trvalý pobyt na päť rokov.
rows.english: I would like to apply for 5-year permanent residence.

rows.label: Reason
rows.english: I am the spouse of a Slovak citizen.

rows.label: Appointment
rows.english: I have an appointment at ten o'clock.

rows.label: Timeline
rows.english: When will the decision be ready?

rows.label: Not clear
rows.english: I don't understand. Can you write it down?

words.slovak: Chcel by som požiadať o trvalý pobyt na päť rokov.
words.english: I would like to apply for 5-year permanent residence.
I am the spouse of a Slovak citizen.
When will the decision be ready?

items.sentence: Chcel by som požiadať o trvalý pobyt na ___ rokov.
items.translation: I would like to apply for permanent residence for ___ years.
choices: tri
štyri
päť
desať

items.sentence: Doklady k žiadosti nesmú byť staršie ako ___ dní.
items.translation: Documents attached to the application cannot be older than ___ days.

items.sentence: Do ___ pracovných dní od prevzatia dokladu o pobyte musí cudzinec uzavrieť zdravotné poistenie.
items.translation: Within ___ working days of receiving the residence document, the foreigner must take out health insurance.

items.sentence: Lekársky posudok musí byť predložený do ___ dní od prevzatia dokladu o pobyte.
items.translation: The medical certificate must be submitted within ___ days of receiving the residence document.

items.sentence: Policajný útvar rozhodne o žiadosti spravidla do ___ dní.
items.translation: The police unit decides on the application usually within ___ days.

items.sentence: Po ___ rokoch s trvalým pobytom na päť rokov možno požiadať o trvalý pobyt na neobmedzený čas.
items.translation: After ___ years with 5-year permanent residence one can apply for unlimited permanent residence.
choices: dva
tri
štyri
päť

scenarios.situation: Your application was submitted with all documents. You are still waiting for a decision after 60 days. Can you stay in Slovakia?
choices: No — you must leave and re-enter
Yes — a complete application allows you to stay legally until a decision is made
Only if you have a valid temporary residence at the same time
Yes — but only for 30 more days
scenarios.answer: Yes — a complete application allows you to stay legally until a decision is made
scenarios.answerMeaning: Once you submit a complete application, you are legally authorised to remain in Slovakia until the decision is made — even if your previous permit expires during that time.

scenarios.situation: You receive your 5-year permanent residence card. What must you do within 3 working days?
choices: Submit your medical certificate
Register at the local council
Take out health insurance
Return your previous residence permit
scenarios.answerMeaning: Within 3 working days of receiving your residence document you must take out health insurance (zdravotné poistenie). This is a legal obligation — do not delay.

scenarios.situation: You received your 5-year permanent residence card 35 days ago. You have not yet submitted your medical certificate. What is your situation?
choices: You are on time — the deadline is 60 days
You are late — the deadline was 30 days
There is no deadline for the medical certificate
You are fine — the deadline only applies to new arrivals
scenarios.answer: You are late — the deadline was 30 days
scenarios.answerMeaning: The medical certificate must be submitted to the Foreign Police within 30 days of receiving your residence document. You can request a 60-day extension — but only before the deadline passes.

scenarios.situation: You need to get the medical certificate. You go to your regular GP. Is this correct?
choices: Yes — any licensed doctor in Slovakia can issue it
No — it must be issued by an approved clinic on the official list
Yes — but only if your GP is registered with the Foreign Police
No — you must go to a state hospital only
scenarios.answer: No — it must be issued by an approved clinic on the official list
scenarios.answerMeaning: The medical certificate confirming you have no public health threatening disease can only be issued by approved clinics listed by the Ministry of Interior. Your regular GP cannot issue this document. Check the reference card for approved clinics in your region.

scenarios.situation: Your accommodation document was issued by a friend who owns the flat where you live. What form must it take?
choices: An email from your friend is enough
A signed statement from your friend confirming they provide accommodation
A notarised statement from your friend AND proof of their ownership of the property
Only a formal lease contract is accepted
scenarios.answer: A notarised statement from your friend AND proof of their ownership of the property
scenarios.answerMeaning: If someone else owns the flat and provides accommodation, they must give a written statement (čestné vyhlásenie) AND provide proof that they are entitled to use the property. The document must cover at least 6 months.

exercises.context: You are applying for 5-year permanent residence as the spouse of a Slovak citizen. You need Martina (your Slovak wife) to prepare her documents confirming her permanent residence in Slovakia. You are texting her.

A.title: Documents sorted! ✅
A.description: Martina knows exactly what to prepare and will have it ready before your appointment.

B.title: You explained it clearly! ✅
B.description: You clarified that her ID card is the key document the Foreign Police need.

nodes.speaker: user
nodes.prompt: Start the conversation. How do you greet Martina?

nodes.speaker: martina
nodes.wrongAnswerTranslation: Hmm... what are you talking about?

choices.text: Žiadam o trvalý pobyt. Potrebujem tvoje doklady.
choices.translation: I am applying for permanent residence. I need your documents.
I have an appointment at the Foreign Police.

nodes.speaker: martina
nodes.text: Jasné! Aké doklady potrebuješ odo mňa?
nodes.translation: Of course! What documents do you need from me?
nodes.wrongAnswerResponse: To nie je doklad... čo presne potrebuješ?
nodes.wrongAnswerTranslation: That's not a document... what exactly do you need?

choices.text: Potvrdenie o trvalom pobyte na Slovensku.
choices.translation: Confirmation of permanent residence in Slovakia.
A valid ID card is enough.

nodes.speaker: martina
nodes.text: Mám platný občiansky preukaz. Stačí to?
nodes.translation: I have a valid ID card. Is that enough?

choices.text: Áno! Občiansky preukaz preukazuje tvoj trvalý pobyt.
choices.translation: Yes! The ID card proves your permanent residence.

choices.text: Nie, potrebujem úradný výpis z evidencie obyvateľov.
choices.translation: No, I need an official extract from the population register.

nodes.speaker: martina
nodes.text: Super. Mám ho pri sebe. Kedy je stretnutie?
nodes.translation: Great. I have it with me. When is the appointment?

nodes.speaker: martina
nodes.text: Dobre. Musím ísť s tebou osobne?
nodes.translation: Alright. Do I need to come with you in person?

choices.text: Áno, prosím. Polícia ťa môže požiadať o overenie.
choices.translation: Yes please. The police may ask to verify with you.

choices.text: Nie je to povinné, ale bolo by to dobré.
choices.translation: It is not mandatory but it would be good.

nodes.speaker: martina
nodes.text: Dobre, prídem s tebou. Veľa šťastia! 🤝
nodes.translation: Alright, I will come with you. Good luck! 🤝

choices.translation: Thank you very much! You are the best.
Why are you coming with me?

nodes.speaker: martina
nodes.text: Dovidenia! Dáme vedieť ako to dopadlo. 🏠
nodes.translation: Goodbye! Let us know how it goes. 🏠

questions.question: What document proves a Slovak citizen's permanent residence in Slovakia?
choices: Their passport
Their valid ID card (občiansky preukaz)
A bank statement
A utility bill
questions.explanation: The Slovak ID card (občiansky preukaz) contains the holder's permanent residence address and is accepted by the Foreign Police as proof. According to the MIC guidance, for marriage-based applications it is sufficient to present the spouse's valid ID card.

questions.question: Does your Slovak spouse need to come to the Foreign Police appointment with you?
choices: Yes — always legally required
No — never required
Not strictly required but recommended — the officer may want to verify
Only if you do not speak Slovak
questions.answer: Not strictly required but recommended — the officer may want to verify
questions.explanation: The law does not require your spouse to attend, but having them present can help if the officer needs to verify details about your marriage or their residence. It is always better to come prepared.

questions.question: Where was the marriage required to take place for it to count as proof of purpose?
choices: Anywhere in the world — marriage is marriage
In your home country
In Slovakia or registered in the Slovak special register
In an EU member state
questions.answer: In Slovakia or registered in the Slovak special register
questions.explanation: For marriage-based permanent residence, the marriage must have taken place in Slovakia or be recorded in the Slovak special register (osobitná matrika). Marriages abroad must be officially registered in Slovakia first.

questions.question: After receiving your 5-year permanent residence card, within how many working days must you take out health insurance?
questions.explanation: Within 3 working days (pracovné dni) of receiving your residence document you must take out health insurance. This is a strict legal obligation — not a recommendation.

scenarios.situation: You received your permanent residence card. You need to get a medical certificate. You book an appointment with your regular GP. Is this correct?
choices: Yes — any registered doctor in Slovakia can issue it
No — only approved clinics on the official Ministry of Interior list can issue it
Yes — but only GPs who speak English
No — you must go to a state university hospital only
scenarios.answer: No — only approved clinics on the official Ministry of Interior list can issue it
scenarios.answerMeaning: The medical certificate confirming no public health threatening disease can only be issued by specific approved clinics. Your regular GP cannot issue this document. See the reference card for the approved clinic in your region.

scenarios.situation: You received your card 28 days ago and have not yet submitted the medical certificate. What should you do?
choices: Nothing — you have 60 days in total
Book the approved clinic urgently — deadline is 30 days
Submit it next month — there is a grace period
Ask your employer to sort it
scenarios.answer: Book the approved clinic urgently — deadline is 30 days
scenarios.answerMeaning: The deadline is 30 days from receiving your residence document. At 28 days you have 2 days left. Book the approved clinic immediately. If you cannot make it in time, you can request a 60-day extension — but do this before the deadline passes.

scenarios.situation: You cannot submit the medical certificate within 30 days due to illness. What can you do?
choices: Nothing — missing the deadline means your permit is cancelled
Request a 60-day extension from the Foreign Police
Submit it whenever you are ready — there are no consequences
Ask your doctor to backdate it
scenarios.answer: Request a 60-day extension from the Foreign Police
scenarios.answerMeaning: You can request a 60-day extension (predĺženie lehoty) from the Foreign Police. You must make this request before the original 30-day deadline. The medical certificate itself must not be older than 30 days when submitted.

scenarios.situation: Your friend already has permanent residence in another EU country and is now getting Slovak permanent residence too. Does he need to submit a medical certificate?
choices: Yes — always required for permanent residence
No — the medical certificate is not required if already holding residence in another EU member state
Yes — but only within 60 days instead of 30
No — only required for non-EU nationals
scenarios.answer: No — the medical certificate is not required if already holding residence in another EU member state
scenarios.answerMeaning: The medical certificate is not required if the applicant already has a granted residence permit in another EU member state, or when changing type of residence within Slovakia.

scenarios.situation: You have held 5-year permanent residence for 4 years. What can you now apply for?
choices: Slovak citizenship directly
Long-term EU residence (dlhodobý pobyt)
Unlimited permanent residence (trvalý pobyt na neobmedzený čas)
Nothing — you must renew the 5-year permit again
scenarios.answer: Unlimited permanent residence (trvalý pobyt na neobmedzený čas)
scenarios.answerMeaning: After 4 years of holding 5-year permanent residence you can apply for unlimited permanent residence (trvalý pobyt na neobmedzený čas). This is the next step — a separate lesson covers this permit.

referenceCard.title: Permanent Residence (5 Years) — Reference Card
referenceCard.titleSlovak: Trvalý pobyt na päť rokov — Referenčná karta
referenceCard.unlockedMessage: 🎉 You completed the Permanent Residence (5 Years) lesson! Your reference card is now saved to your profile.

checklist.item: Completed application form (official MV SR form)

checklist.warning: Bring original + photocopy of ALL pages
For marriage: place of marriage + spouse's valid Slovak ID card. Marriage must be in Slovakia or registered in Slovak special register.
From your home country AND any country you lived in 90+ days during 6 consecutive months in the last 3 years. Cannot be older than 90 days.
Lease contract OR owner's written statement + proof of ownership OR accommodation facility confirmation. Must cover at least 6 months.
Required from the parent who has visitation rights but is not the primary guardian

afterApproval.action: Submit medical certificate (lekársky posudok) to the Foreign Police — only from an approved clinic. Certificate must not be older than 30 days. Extension of 60 days possible on request.

keyFacts: Documents cannot be older than 90 days — except civil registry documents (e.g. marriage certificate)
All foreign documents must be apostilled AND translated by a certified Slovak translator (Czech translation also accepted)
Incomplete application is not accepted — you receive a written list of missing documents
Complete application = you can legally stay in Slovakia while waiting for the decision
Medical certificate can only be issued by approved clinics — not your regular GP
After 4 years with this permit you can apply for unlimited permanent residence
